comment: very bad results for this simple task only a curve with pipe effect

conversation.content: Workspace cleared.
make a curve and divide it in points and place a sphere on each point and have a random radius between twentyth and tenth length of the curve
A 3D serpentine curve with spheres of random radius (1/20 to 1/10 of curve length) placed at each division point
Structural validation error (does not count against repair budget, 1/3) — asking the model to fix.

The IR compiler left Expression inputs (a,b) and PointsFromLists inputs (x,y,z,scale) unwired. I need to connect the curveLength slider to each Expression's 'a' input, the Series 't' to each Expression's 'b' input, and each Expression output to the corresponding PointsFromLists input. The ListConstant nodes are redundant — the slider feeds 'a' directly — so I remove them.
Structural validation error (does not count against repair budget, 2/3) — asking the model to fix.

The IR compiler left all Expression a/b inputs and PointsFromLists x/y/z/scale inputs unwired, and my previous edge additions had no effect. I'll do a full rebuild with formulas that reference slider names directly (so only the Series list needs wiring to each Expression's 'b' input), and explicitly wire every edge including targetHandle for non-default ports.
Structural validation error (does not count against repair budget, 3/3) — asking the model to fix.

My added edges were silently dropped in both previous attempts — the canvas still shows only the 4 original edges. The problem is likely that sourceHandle must also be explicitly specified for non-default handles. Series outputs 'values' and Expression outputs 'value', so I need to include both sourceHandle and targetHandle on every edge.
Structural validation error (exemptions exhausted, counts against repair budget) — asking the model to fix.

Issues detected (auto-repair 1/2): Expression "x_x" uses "b" in formula "curveLength * b" but that per-element input is not connected — a/b/c/d are supplied by wiring a number list into the matching input handle, NOT by a slider. Add the edge: connect a Series/Range/Expression list into "b". Left unwired it evaluates as 0 and collapse dependent positions.; Expression "y_x" uses "b" in formula "curveLength * sin(b * 6.283) * 0.18" but that per-element input is not connected — a/b/c/d are supplied by wiring a number list into the matching input handle, NOT by a slider. Add the edge: connect a Series/Range/Expression list into "b". Left unwired it evaluates as 0 and collapse dependent positions.; Expression "z_x" uses "b" in formula "curveLength * cos(b * 6.283) * 0.12 + curveLength * 0.15" but that per-element input is not connected — a/b/c/d are supplied by wiring a number list into the matching input handle, NOT by a slider. Add the edge: connect a Series/Range/Expression list into "b". Left unwired it evaluates as 0 and collapse dependent positions.
Error: Ollama Error: 500 Internal Server Error
there is pipe but no spheres
AI (Ollama (Local) (glm-5.2:cloud)) is working…

The radii_x Expression computes the per-point random radius (curveLength/20 to curveLength/10) but its output was never connected to the PointsFromLists 'scale' input, so InstanceOnPoints placed unit-radius spheres that are nearly invisible next to the pipe. Adding one edge: radii_x → pts(param:scale).
Structural validation error (does not count against repair budget, 1/3) — asking the model to fix.

The Series 't' list was never wired to the Expression 'b' inputs, and the Expression outputs were never wired to PointsFromLists x/y/z/scale — so all points collapsed to origin with unit scale. Adding all 8 missing edges with explicit sourceHandle and targetHandle.
Structural validation error (does not count against repair budget, 2/3) — asking the model to fix.
A 3D serpentine curve with spheres of random radius (1/20 to 1/10 of curve length) placed at each division point
Structural validation error (does not count against repair budget, 3/3) — asking the model to fix.
